2604.15958.title: A Case Study on the Impact of Anonymization Along the RAG Pipeline
2604.15958.paper_summary: Despite the considerable promise of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), many real-world use cases may create privacy concerns, where the purported utility of RAG-enabled insights comes at the risk of exposing private information to either the LLM or the end user requesting the response. As a potential mitigation, using anonymization techniques to remove personally identifiable information (PII) and other sensitive markers in the underlying data represents a practical and sensible course of action for RAG administrators. Despite a wealth of literature on the topic, no works consider the placement of anonymization along the RAG pipeline, i.e., asking the question, where should anonymization happen? In this case study, we systematically and empirically measure the impact of anonymization at two important points along the RAG pipeline: the dataset and generated answer. We show that differences in privacy-utility trade-offs can be observed depending on where anonymization took place, demonstrating the significance of privacy risk mitigation placement in RAG.
2604.15958.paper_summary_zh: 儘管檢索增強生成（RAG）具有相當大的潛力，但許多現實世界的使用案例可能會引發隱私問題，因為RAG所提供的見解的實用性可能會冒著將私人信息暴露給大型語言模型（LLM）或請求回應的最終用戶的風險。作為一種潛在的緩解措施，使用匿名化技術來移除個人識別信息（PII）和其他敏感標記，代表了RAG管理者可行且合理的行動方案。儘管在這個主題上有大量文獻，但沒有研究考慮匿名化在RAG管道中的位置，即提出問題：匿名化應該在何處進行？在這個案例研究中，我們系統性且實證地測量匿名化在RAG管道兩個重要點的影響：數據集和生成的答案。我們顯示出，根據匿名化發生的地點，可以觀察到隱私與效用之間的權衡差異，這證明了在RAG中隱私風險緩解位置的重要性。
2604.15958.author: Andreea-Elena Bodea et.al.
2604.15958.authors: Andreea-Elena Bodea, Stephen Meisenbacher, Florian Matthes
